description_long: This bungalow features a single-story structure with a broad, gabled roof and a combination of wood siding and shingle textures. The front facade includes a set of double glass doors opening onto a small wooden porch, while the rear side displays a more private, windowed elevation. The side view reveals a garage integrated into the lower level, with a driveway and visible garage doors. The roof is cross-gabled, providing architectural interest and additional attic space. The windows are arranged in a mostly symmetrical pattern, with grouped windows on the sides and rear. The property is set on a rectangular grassy plot bordered by a low retaining wall, with a few vertical fence panels visible on one side.
description: A single-story wooden bungalow with a cross-gabled roof, front porch, and integrated garage, set on a grassy lot with symmetrical window arrangements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with double glass doors, a small wooden porch, and the broad gabled roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the integrated garage, driveway, and grouped windows, as well as the cross-gabled roof structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a private elevation with symmetrical windows and the continuation of the gabled roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a secondary entrance, additional windows, and the grassy lot extending around the house.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with grouped side and rear windows
